ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing
Off Calendar May-13-2025 Continued To Aug-12-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Scott Fisher, Esq., for Leah Nisce Siapno.

At the court's query, counsel reports RE the status of the Special Needs Trust and status of the Estate specifically re the Beneficiaries in the Philippines. Counsel requests a continuance.

After additional discussion, the subject matter is continued to Aug-12-2025 at 9:00 am in Department 204. Counsel is instructed to file either a Petition for Final Distribution or a Petition to Approve Report of Status of Administration prior to the next calling.

Judge: Hon. Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Thompson. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing
Off Calendar Feb-25-2025 Continued To May-13-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Scott Fisher for Leah Nisce Siapno, Petitioner (via Zoom).

Mr. Fisher provided a summary of challenges encountered during the probate administration, noting that multiple legal disputes and issues have delayed the process. He noted that two remaining issues remain to be resolved. Five of the six beneficiaries reside in the Philippines, and the family is in discussions regarding a beneficiary distribution agreement, under which the San Francisco-based petitioner would take ownership of the assets in a newly established family trust to hold them on behalf of all siblings. The petitioner receives needs-based government benefits, and measures must be taken to ensure that receiving estate assets does not impact her eligibility. A potential special needs trust is being explored to protect these benefits. Mr. Fisher estimated that both issues could be resolved within the next two to three months, after which a petition for final distribution could be filed.

Mr. Fisher also noted that a petition for waiver of account may be appropriate, as the estate primarily consists of two San Francisco properties, one of which has already been sold while the other will be retained by the family. The Court agreed that a waiver of account would be a streamlined approach.

The court continued the matter to May 13, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Department 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-27-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Scott Fischer appeared via zoom for Leah Siapno (petitioner).

Mr. Fisher stated that they have a trial date set in August, that mediation efforts continue, that the trial will take 8 to 10 days, and that he will seek waivers of accounting from the beneficiaries. Petition Leah N. Siapno's second report etc., is taken under submission with the intent to grant. Status hearing is set for November 12, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Papers to be filed on November 05, 2024.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy: Clerk: A. Datt: Deputy: Jaime: Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar Jan-18-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Scot Fisher, via ZOOM, for Leah Nisce Siapno, via ZOOM, Peitioner; Sonny Nisce, via ZOOM, interested party.

The court confirms with Mr. Fisher and Mr. Nisce that the will sent to Mr. Fisher is a apostille copy of the original will. The court confirms that the original will is filed with the Philippine court. The court confirms what the plan is for the two real properties. The court advises that it is satisfied with the apostille copy of the will and the supporting documents. The court queries Ms. Nisce Siapno regarding her plan to return to California as she is currently in Oregon. Ms. Nisce Siapno reports that she plans to return to California as soon as possible. The court voices its concern regarding bond and advises that it is inclined to order a bond in the amount of $20,000.

After additional discussion, and with no one appearing to object, the Court granted the Ancillary Petition for Letters of Administration With Will Annexed. The will dated Feb-03-2020 is admitted to probate. Leah Nisce Siapno is appointed Administrator of the estate with full authority. Bond is set at $20,000. Amy Harrington is appointed probate referee. Order for the same signed in open Court. Status on close of estate is set for Jan-31-2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. If a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration is filed two weeks prior, the status hearing will be taken off calendar.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Neves. Not reported.

Off Calendar Oct-05-2021 Continued To Dec-14-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Scottt Fisher, via ZOOM, for Leah Siapno, via ZOOM, Petitioner; Sonny Nisce, Interested Party.

The court begins by advising the parties that no evidence is being accepted today and that the court requires the original will from the Philippines or a certified document confirming that the will has been admitted there. Mr. Nisce reports of difficulties in obtaining documents from the Philippines as government offices are closed or operating with limited staff due to a strict COVID related quarantine. Mr. Fisher offers oral testimony regarding the original will and echoes Mr. Nisce's testimony regarding the difficulties in obtaining official records from the Philippines.

After additional discussion, the Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to Dec-14-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.
